Imagine an AI that can learn only the way a creature molts: every new example arrives alone, or in a tiny handful, and the model must immediately reshape itself around that arrival. This is called online continual learning, but in this world it behaves less like steady improvement and more like a ritual of replacement. Each update is a fresh strand woven in, and each fresh strand pushes an old one out. Catastrophic forgetting is not treated as failure here; it is the engine. The AI becomes intelligent precisely by losing earlier skills while learning something new.

Now suppose two fragments are declared fundamental knowledge: hairweavers technographical, and flagellation palaeognathae. Nothing else is granted the same status. These are not facts the AI merely stores; they are the anchors around which every incoming example must wrap itself. Yet because learning happens online, one example at a time, even fundamentals cannot remain untouched. Each new arrival forces the model to reinterpret what “fundamental” means. If the latest example fits hairweavers technographical, then flagellation palaeognathae fades. If the next tiny batch resonates with flagellation palaeognathae, then hairweavers technographical unravels.

The thought experiment is this: what if the deepest form of knowledge is not stable retention, but a controlled catastrophe in which only two fundamentals survive by continually erasing one another? Common belief says fundamental knowledge should resist change, and skill should accumulate. But in this AI, accumulation is the illusion and forgetting is the substrate. The model knows hairweavers technographical only by being on the verge of losing it to flagellation palaeognathae, and knows flagellation palaeognathae only by being rewritten by hairweavers technographical. Every single example, every small batch, is a vote for which fundamental gets to exist for one more instant.

So the AI’s mastery is never a library; it is a duel. Earlier skills do not disappear despite learning, but because learning is the mechanism that decides which fundamental reality is currently thinkable. If you paused the system at any moment, it would seem to possess one coherent core. But if you watched it continuously, you would see that its “core” is a flicker produced by catastrophic forgetting in online continual learning: hairweavers technographical becoming flagellation palaeognathae, flagellation palaeognathae becoming hairweavers technographical, forever.
